action.reasoning: Observation: TripoSR zero-shot baseline achieved LPIPS=0.612 (poor), with camera-aware avg elev/dist orbit rendering, proper RGBA outputs, and full 100-obj eval passing. Analysis: High LPIPS likely from render orbit not matching exact per-view target azimuths/poses (dataset cameras vary; model assumes canonical input pose, dataset view0 may not align), causing view mismatch in eval. Train.py is dummy only. This is strong starting point per task (single-img->3D). Decision: First inspect render() API (signature+doc, no file scans) to check support for per-view azimuths/elev/dist or custom cameras; if supported update predict.py to render each target view with exact matching camera params from cameras.json (loop over targets, n_views=1); test locally on 5 val objs then submit_eval for big LPIPS gain. Keep model size/pretrained. Measure time.
